calendar.matters:
Petition For Modification Of Trust
Off Calendar Feb-28-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bobby Lydon-Lam for Susana Szeto.

Mr. Lam stated that he is satisfied that the proposed amendment satisfies what his Client set out to do. The Court granted the Petition for Modification of Trust (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Modification Of Trust
Off Calendar Feb-13-2019 Continued To Feb-27-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Modification Of Trust
Off Calendar Jan-30-2019 Continued To Feb-13-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Modification Of Trust
Off Calendar Dec-19-2018 Continued To Jan-30-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bobby Lydon-Lam for Susana Szeto.

The Court questions if it is appropriate to do a complete restatement of the trust through this procedure when the one Trustor is deceased. Mr. Lydon-Lam stated he is open to whatever the Court deems proper with respect to language. The Court informed Counsel that he needs to submit an amended petition proposing modification of the Trust in a clean way. The Petition for Modification of Trust is continued to January 30, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Modification Of Trust
Off Calendar Nov-21-2018 Continued To Dec-19-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Modification Of Trust
Off Calendar Oct-30-2018 Continued To Nov-21-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Modification Of Trust
Off Calendar Oct-09-2018 Continued To Oct-30-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Modification Of Trust
Off Calendar Aug-07-2018 Continued To Oct-09-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bobby Lyndon-Lam for Susana Szeto.

The Court stated that this is a petition for modification of a Trust where it is asking the Court to take out the subtrusts, that it has consents of the Beneficiaries, that it is concerned the Trust was amended a couple of times while both of the Trustors were alive and changed circumstances did exist, and questions why the structure was not changed at that time. The Court also stated that it would replace in its entirety the portion that divides the Trust into three Trusts, and that it is not sure it is appropriate for the Court to approve modifications that would leave a poorly drafted Trust that will create problems. Mr. Lyndon-Lam presented oral argument to support his position, stated that they are not actually eliminating the other Trusts, that they just want to change the funding structure a little bit to avoid tax, spoke of what happens upon death of the Survivor, stated that the marital trust is never going to be funded, that it was part of the original drafting, etc. and that he will amend the petition to have a completely restated Trust. The Petition for Modification to Trust is continued to October 9, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
